##NAMES=Andreybulakhite
##RRUFFID=R250014
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ni(C_2_O_4_)·2H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Nyud-II Cu–Ni sulfide deposit, Monchegorsk mafic–ultramafic pluton, Kola Peninsula, Russia
##OWNER=Mineralogical Museum of the Department of Mineralogy, Saint Petersburg State University, Saint Petersburg, Russia
##SOURCE=Oleg S. Vereshchagin
##DESCRIPTION=Platy to prismatic crystals found in lichen colonies that overgrow the oxidized surfaces of pyrrhotite–pentlandite–chalcopyrite ore. This is the type sample reported in Vereshchagin et al (2025)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250014

##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Fragment in Debye-Scherrer geometry
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 11.8392 b: 5.3312 c: 9.8357 alpha: 90 beta: 126.723 gamma: 90 volume: 497.59 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=785
